Module 6:
JV Launch, Part 2
!
Executive Summary
How do you build relationships with JV partners? It’s all about adding
value…
!
Here are some ways to do that:
• 	Buy their product - use it, and tell them how you used it
• 	Leave intelligent comments on their blog - and read their blog so you get
to learn more about them
• 	Comment on their social media - add to the conversation
• 	Write about them on your blog
• 	When someone sees that they’ve been written about they become
curious and will want to see who wrote it
• 	Sell their stuff - join their affiliate program and do a great job - this is a
way to “buy your way in”
• 	What do they want? What interests do they have? Find a way to add
value through that - here are some examples:
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 1 of 2 --

• 	Mike Filsaime offered me a telephone bridge line during a time of
need - this act of kindness created our relationship
• 	Erico (a Platinum member) bought 700 copies of Brendon Burchard’s
book for all his clients - I naturally told Brendon about it myself
• 	“The JV Food Chain” - it’s tough to get on the calendars and radar of the
A-players
• 	Imagine that the JV “food chain” is divided into A, B, C, D, and E
• 	You can start further down the food chain (with the E players), build a
relationship, and then get introduced to the D players
• 	You can continue to work your way up the food chain
• 	This is a long-term process - it doesn't happen overnight but it does
happen and it’s completely worth it
!
2	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
